input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-05T00:59:54.214Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** ÔÇö use `AppThemeBinding` to supply values for `Light`, `Dark`, and optionally `Default`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to system theme changes."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework detects `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (`Light` or `Dark`) and automatically re-evaluates bindings when the user toggles their OS theme ÔÇö no event wiring needed.

---

**C# equivalent** ÔÇö use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:

```csharp
// For Color properties (shorthand)
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For any property (generic version)
myPage.SetAppTheme<Color>(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Colors.White,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

myButton.SetAppTheme<Color>(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

---

**Key points:**

| | XAML | C# |
|---|---|---|
| Syntax | `{AppThemeBinding Light=ÔÇª, Dark=ÔÇª}` | `.SetAppTheme<T>()` / `.SetAppThemeColor()` |
| Auto-updates on theme change | Ô£à | Ô£à |
| Works with any bindable property | Ô£à (images, strings, etc.) | Ô£à |

**Tip:** For larger apps, define theme colors as resources in `App.xaml` and reference them, rather than scattering hex codes:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>

<!-- Usage -->
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{StaticResource PageBg}" />
```
